claim: Context engineering is prompt engineering for AI applications (agents, not chatbots); it involves designing systems to deliver the right information in the right format at the right time, not iterative back-and-forth conversation.
actionable: For AI applications, shift from thinking of prompts as conversational to thinking of them as instruction manuals that cover all actions, scenarios, and resources an agent needs without iterative refinement.

claim: AI agents require six essential components: model, tools, knowledge/memory, audio/speech, guardrails, and orchestration; context engineering unifies these by providing the instruction manual for how they fit together.
actionable: When building AI agents, ensure your system prompt documents all six components (model choice, tools available, memory access, speech capabilities, safety constraints, monitoring) and how they integrate.

claim: When building multi-agent systems, always share context between agents and carefully manage decision points because actions carry implicit decisions that downstream agents depend on.
actionable: In multi-agent systems, explicitly design context passing between agents and document where decisions occur to avoid agents making conflicting or dependent decisions without shared context.

claim: Practical context engineering uses XML tags and markdown to structure prompts for clarity and reduces human-agent iteration by frontloading all necessary instructions, constraints, and resources.
actionable: Structure agent prompts using XML tags (e.g., <role>, <task>, <constraints>) and markdown for organization; this improves agent comprehension and reduces need for conversational refinement.

claim: Context engineering strategies include writing context (agents document decisions), selecting context (pull from external sources), compressing context (reduce token usage), and isolating context (split across environments).
actionable: When engineering context for agents, use compression techniques (summarization, filtering), isolation strategies (separate sensitive data), and selection methods (retrieve only relevant sources) to optimize token usage.
